summary: ISS-1750 alleged the SEC-4.2.7a NN-tour upper-end '16,400 km' overstated reality (claimed true value ~15,200 km). Two independent facts close it: (1) the literal '16,400 km' wording was already replaced by EXP-268/EXP-276 - section now reads '12,400 km from Canary up to ~18,800 km from Eskdalemuir'; (2) independent recomputation of NN tours under AE-from-N disc projection confirms Eskdalemuir-start tour = 18,811 km (matches curmudgeon SEC-4.2.7a.c1 P3-longest-NN-tour-understated calculation and matches the integrated 18,800 figure). ISS-1750's '15,200 km' matches no plausible convention tested (4 conventions: AE-from-N disc, AE-from-S disc, haversine globe, closed-tour AE-from-N) - the issue's recomputation appears to be itself wrong. Subsequent c2/c3 reviews (verdict_holds=true) accepted 18,800 without re-flag. Close with no patch, no test, no rebuild needed. Note: this EXP authored under disk-pressure (clone disk full, ENOSPC); EXP file written via FUSE-direct, workspace-sync universal-pusher will copy to git next cycle.
